name: Norway (naval ensign)

colors.symbolism: the field of the national flag

description: A swallow-tailed and tongued (tre-tunget) version of the Norwegian flag — a red field with a blue Nordic cross fimbriated white — used as the state and naval ensign.
history: The Norwegian naval ensign and state flag is a swallow-tailed-and-tongued version of the national flag, established in 1905 (re-confirmed under the Royal Decree of 17 February 1905) when Norway dissolved the union with Sweden. Used by the Royal Norwegian Navy.

features.symbolism: Christianity and ties to the other Nordic nations
